raw_title: Discourse Game of Educational Policy: Framing Competition Among Stakeholders in Policy Communication
raw_abstract: This paper explores the central role of communication in informing the education policy with a particular interest in how different stakeholders of the education sector, that is, governments, educators, students, and the society in general use competitive framing to alter the population opinion and how they shape the processes of making of policies. The study examines how these actors are using the process of narrative construction in order to integrate educational issues with their interests which is done through a systematic literature review (SLR). The findings emphasize the fact that communication is not just a medium through which policy is passed, but it is dynamic in nature where policy is constructed through discourse. Specific focus is given to such values as equity and efficiency which tend to emerge in such framings, particularly, in those of social media-based public discourse. Social media sites enhance the power of the stakeholders to influence the mainstream discourses, hence, controlling the policy debates trend. The study sheds light on ways in which discourse framing can be used in policy communication to provide guidance to policy makers on how they can become aware of competing interests. In the end, this piece of work enhances our comprehension of the impact of strategic communication on the policy debate in education and it also emphasized the importance of inclusive and balanced discourse in the policy making process.
title_en: Discourse Game of Educational Policy: Framing Competition Among Stakeholders in Policy Communication
title_zh: 教育政策的话语博弈：政策沟通中利益相关者的框架竞争
abstract_en: This paper explores the central role of communication in informing the education policy with a particular interest in how different stakeholders of the education sector, that is, governments, educators, students, and the society in general use competitive framing to alter the population opinion and how they shape the processes of making of policies. The study examines how these actors are using the process of narrative construction in order to integrate educational issues with their interests which is done through a systematic literature review (SLR). The findings emphasize the fact that communication is not just a medium through which policy is passed, but it is dynamic in nature where policy is constructed through discourse. Specific focus is given to such values as equity and efficiency which tend to emerge in such framings, particularly, in those of social media-based public discourse. Social media sites enhance the power of the stakeholders to influence the mainstream discourses, hence, controlling the policy debates trend. The study sheds light on ways in which discourse framing can be used in policy communication to provide guidance to policy makers on how they can become aware of competing interests. In the end, this piece of work enhances our comprehension of the impact of strategic communication on the policy debate in education and it also emphasized the importance of inclusive and balanced discourse in the policy making process.
abstract_zh: 本文探讨了沟通在教育政策制定中的核心作用，特别关注教育部门的不同利益相关者（即政府、教育工作者、学生和整个社会）如何利用竞争性框架来改变公众意见，以及他们如何影响政策制定过程。该研究通过系统文献综述（SLR）考察了这些参与者如何利用叙事构建过程将教育问题与自身利益相结合。研究结果强调，沟通不仅仅是政策传递的媒介，而且具有动态性，政策是通过话语构建的。特别关注了公平和效率等价值观，这些价值观往往出现在此类框架中，尤其是在基于社交媒体的公共讨论中。社交媒体增强了利益相关者影响主流话语的能力，从而控制了政策辩论的趋势。该研究揭示了话语框架如何在政策沟通中发挥作用，为政策制定者如何意识到竞争利益提供指导。最后，这项工作加深了我们对战略沟通对教育政策辩论影响的理解，并强调了包容性和平衡性话语在政策制定过程中的重要性。
plain_summary_zh: 这篇论文研究了在教育政策制定过程中，不同群体（如政府、老师、学生和公众）如何通过讲故事和强调不同观点来影响大家的看法，从而改变政策方向。研究发现，沟通不仅仅是传递信息，而是通过讨论来共同构建政策。特别是在社交媒体上，人们更容易表达自己的观点，影响政策讨论。研究提醒政策制定者要注意这些不同的声音，确保政策讨论公平包容。
plain_summary_en: This paper examines how different groups involved in education (like governments, teachers, students, and the public) use storytelling and emphasize different perspectives to shape public opinion and influence policy decisions. The research shows that communication isn't just about sharing information—it's how policies are actually created through discussion. Social media gives people more power to voice their views and steer policy debates. The study advises policymakers to pay attention to these competing voices to ensure fair and inclusive policy discussions.
research_direction_zh: 探索沟通策略和话语框架在公共政策制定中的作用，特别是在教育领域，分析利益相关者如何通过叙事和媒体影响政策进程。
research_direction_en: Investigating the role of communication strategies and discourse framing in public policy-making, particularly in education, analyzing how stakeholders use narratives and media to influence policy processes.
